conditionDescriptions: Chronic kidney disease with eGFR less than 45 mL/min per 1.73 m²
contraindications.clinicalRationale: Thiazolidinediones should be discontinued when kidney function deteriorates below eGFR 45 mL/min per 1.73 m² based on KDIGO diabetes in CKD treatment algorithms
contraindications.sourceSnippet: Figure 23: Sulfonylurea — Discontinue when eGFR < 30; Dialysis: Discontinue. TZD (thiazolidinedione) — Discontinue when eGFR < 45; Dialysis: Discontinue.

contraindications.clinicalRationale: Thiazolidinediones must be discontinued in patients receiving dialysis therapy as indicated in KDIGO treatment algorithms
contraindications.sourceSnippet: Figure 23: Sulfonylurea — Discontinue when eGFR < 30; Dialysis: Discontinue. TZD (thiazolidinedione) — Discontinue when eGFR < 45; Dialysis: Discontinue.

contraindications.clinicalRationale: Thiazolidinediones are considered less suitable for patients with heart failure according to KDIGO treatment guidance
contraindications.sourceSnippet: Figure 25 | For patients with heart failure: GLP-1 RA is more suitable; thiazolidinedione is less suitable.
